questions: At **CyanGate LLC**, you led the development of the **JSON-to-JSON AI Agentic System**, which involved integrating multiple LLM providers like OpenAI, Copilot, Gemini, and Anthropic—only to later optimize the system to run with a *single LLM* while cutting costs and latency by 50%. This likely required scrapping or heavily revising prior work. Walk me through how you **managed your own frustration or attachment to the initial approach**, how you communicated this pivot to your team or stakeholders, and what specific feedback or data convinced you this was the right direction. *How do you balance pride in your work with the need to abandon it when the business or technical landscape shifts?*

During your **Atlassian Jira Intelligence System** project at CyanGate, you built a hybrid search architecture using **Copilot Studio, Power Automate, and Azure AI Search**—only to note in your CV that this was completed *‘before MCP’* and required ‘different integration approaches at the time.’ This suggests the solution became obsolete or required rework due to platform updates. Describe a moment where **a tool, API, or framework you relied on was deprecated, replaced, or fundamentally changed mid-project**. How did you **re-prioritize your tasks**, and what did you do to ensure your team or clients didn’t lose trust in the timeline or outcome? *What’s your strategy for staying agile when the ground shifts beneath you?*

You’ve competed in high-stakes AI challenges like **TEKNOFEST’s NLP and E-Commerce competitions**, where you had to pivot strategies based on leaderboard feedback or last-minute rule changes. Now, imagine you join Zephlen and, after weeks of designing an agentic workflow for a client, the **product team suddenly shifts priorities to a different vertical**—requiring you to abandon your current work entirely. *How would you react emotionally in the first 24 hours?* Then, practically, how would you **reallocate your focus, leverage any reusable components from the old project, and align with the new goal without resentment**? Give me an example from your past where you’ve had to ‘kill your darlings’ and how it turned out.
